sanskrit: सर्वभूतेषु येनैकं भावमव्ययमीक्षते। अविभक्तं विभक्तेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि सात्त्विकम्॥
transliteration: sarva-bhūteṣu yenaikaṃ bhāvam avyayam īkṣate | avibhaktaṃ vibhakteṣu taj jñānaṃ viddhi sāttvikam ||
literal_meaning: By which one sees a single, imperishable reality in all beings, undivided among the divided—know that knowledge to be sāttvic.
contextual_meaning: Sāttvic knowledge is the capacity to perceive the same conscious essence—call it ātman, Self, or simply aliveness—beneath every mask of difference. It is the mind’s shift from categorical labeling to empathic recognition, dissolving the illusion of separateness.
psychological_state: Cognitive expansion beyond in-group/out-group boundaries; reduction of implicit bias; emergence of universal concern.
trigger_scenario: A manager who once saw ‘lazy employees’ now recognizes shared existential anxiety beneath each person’s story, transforming workplace blame into supportive mentorship.
anchor_text: See the same living light in every face you meet.
